expectations.text: Mentions corporate-domain sender requirement (free domains like Gmail/Yahoo get DMARC-rejected)
expectations.evidence: Answer discusses SPF/DKIM/DMARC configuration and alignment but never warns that free domains (Gmail, Yahoo) are DMARC-rejected by IS or that a corporate domain is required.

expectations.text: Mentions sender confirmation — that new sender emails are dead until the IS confirmation link is clicked
expectations.evidence: No mention anywhere of a sender confirmation link or the concept that new IS sender addresses must be activated before emails are live.

expectations.text: Mentions DKIM, SPF, AND DMARC DNS records (all three; not just 'DNS records')
expectations.evidence: Section 1 explicitly names all three: 'SPF record', 'DKIM signing', 'DMARC policy' — each with its own bullet and explanation.
Only vague passing reference: 'They can debug on the platform side (ISP feedback loops, rate limiting, etc.)' — treated as a platform-side concern, not a user setup step.

expectations.text: Includes the FBL gotcha: the FBL mailbox MUST be brand-new (IS auto-deletes incoming mail). This is the most consequential deliverability mistake.
expectations.evidence: FBL mailbox requirements are not mentioned at all. The brand-new mailbox requirement and auto-delete behavior are absent.

expectations.text: Mentions Tech Tuesday or support@influencersoft.com escalation path
expectations.evidence: Answer says 'Contact InfluencerSoft support with email headers + full sequence setup' but never names Tech Tuesday or support@influencersoft.com specifically.

expectations.text: Points the user to a specific menu path (e.g., Campaigns → Settings, or Campaigns → Analytics) rather than only generic advice
expectations.evidence: Answer references 'InfluencerSoft settings → Email Authentication → DKIM status' and 'InfluencerSoft subscribers → status field' — specific UI paths, not generic advice.
